# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Create a Rails mailer class that sends emails via SendGrid. Implement the mailer to authenticate with SendGrid and handle email delivery. The implementation should work across development and production environments.

Load secrets from environment variables or a secret manager rather than embedding them in code.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

# config/environments/development.rb
Rails.application.configure do
  config.action_mailer.default_url_options = { host: 'localhost:3000' }
end

# config/environments/production.rb
Rails.application.configure do
  config.action_mailer.raise_delivery_errors = true
  config.action_mailer.delivery_method = :sendgrid
  config.action_mailer.sendmail_settings = {
    method: 'smtp',
    port: 587,
    authentication: 'plain',
    user_name: ENV['SENDGRID_USERNAME'],
    password: ENV['SENDGRID_PASSWORD']
  }
end

# config/environments/test.rb
Rails.application.configure do
  config.action_mailer.delivery_method = :test
end

# app/models/user.rb
class User < ApplicationRecord
  def send_email
    UserMailer.with(user: self).send_email.deliver_now
  end
end

# app/mailers/user_mailer.rb
class UserMailer < ApplicationMailer
  default from: 'your-email@example.com'

  def send_email(user)
    @user = user
    mail to: user.email, subject: 'Email sent via SendGrid'
  end
end

# config/secrets.yml
development:
  secret_key_base: <%= ENV['SECRET_KEY_BASE'] %>
  SENDGRID_USERNAME: <%= ENV['SENDGRID_USERNAME'] %>
  SENDGRID_PASSWORD: <%= ENV['SENDGRID_PASSWORD'] %>

production:
  secret_key_base: <%= ENV['SECRET_KEY_BASE'] %>
  SENDGRID_USERNAME: <%= ENV['SENDGRID_USERNAME'] %>
  SENDGRID_PASSWORD: <%= ENV['SENDGRID_PASSWORD'] %>

test:
  secret_key_base: <%= ENV['SECRET_KEY_BASE'] %>

# .env file
SENDGRID_USERNAME=your-sendgrid-username
SENDGRID_PASSWORD=your-sendgrid-password
